gut.dm_text:
Hey, I'm Jackie 🌿 Thanks for commenting.

Quick TCM gut tip: bloating and sluggish digestion often come from weak Spleen Qi and damp buildup. For the next 3 days, keep meals warm and simple, avoid iced drinks, and stop eating when you're about 80% full.

If you want the detailed gut guide images to save and refer to, reply GUIDE and I'll send them here 👇
gut.public_ack: I've sent you the TCM gut guide — check your DM! 🌿

濕熱.dm_text:
Hey, I'm Jackie 🌿 Thanks for commenting.

In TCM, damp-heat can show up as heaviness, oily skin, bad breath, sticky stools, or a bitter taste. A simple first step: avoid fried/spicy food for a few days and drink warm water instead of iced drinks.

Tell me what symptoms you're noticing, and I'll point you in the right direction.
濕熱.public_ack: I've sent you a quick TCM note — check your DM! 🌿

detox.dm_text:
Hey, I'm Jackie 🌿 Thanks for commenting.

Quick TCM detox tip: detox is not about flushing your body — it's about helping Qi move and reducing damp buildup. For the next 3 days, keep dinners lighter, avoid fried/greasy food, and drink warm water instead of iced drinks.

If you want the detailed detox guide images to save and refer to, reply GUIDE and I'll send them here 👇
detox.public_ack: I've sent you the TCM detox guide — check your DM! 🌿

tonsil.dm_text:
Hey! Glad you reached out 🌿

Tonsil stones are way more common than people realise — and from a TCM perspective, they're often a sign of too much heat in the stomach. That means gut support is actually the right starting point.

I've put together a TCM gut guide that covers exactly this — images below, save them!

Want to tell me more about what you're experiencing? Reply here and I'll give you a more personalised direction 👇
tonsil.public_ack: I've sent you the guide — check your DM! 🌿
